session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8589 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified PR argument.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill following the instructions. First, I'll fetch the PR feedback using the specified PR number:

## ❌ Dignified Code Simplifier Review

</details>", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8589#issuecomment-3981559953" }, { "id": 3981560571, "author": "github-actions[bot]", "body": "<!-- tripwires-review -->

## ✅ Tripwires Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-01 17:39:39 PT

No tripwire violations detected. The error handling changes follow documented CLI patterns correctly.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Tripwires Triggered
- **CLI Error Handling Anti-Patterns** → loaded docs/learned/cli/error-handling-antipatterns.md

### Tier 1 Pattern Matches
All universal tripwire patterns checked:
✅ bare subprocess.run with check=True - None found
✅ adding methods to Gateway ABCs - None found
✅ raising exceptions for expected failures - None found
✅ Path.home() direct usage - None found
✅ os.chdir() calls - None found
✅ time module imports - None found
✅ I/O in __init__ - None found
✅ gh pr diff --name-only usage - None found
✅ session IDs via environment variables - None found

### Tier 2 Semantic Matches
✅ **Using RuntimeError for expected CLI failures** - COMPLIANT

The code correctly CATCHES RuntimeError from the gateway layer and converts it to UserFacingCliError with actionable messages. This follows the migration pattern documented in error-handling-antipatterns.md:
- Detects known error patterns (404/Not Found)
- Provides actionable remediation (erk init capability add command)
- Preserves exception chain with `from e`

### Violations Summary
None.

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py`: 0 violations

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-01 17:39:39 PT**: No tripwire violations detected. Error handling follows documented CLI patterns correctly.

</details>", "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8589#issuecomment-3981560571"

"body": "<!-- dignified-python-review -->

## ✅ Dignified Python Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-01 17:40:19 PT

No violations detected. Code follows dignified Python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ Exception handling - Compliant error boundary with context
✅ Exception chaining - Proper B904 pattern with `from e`
✅ String inspection - Valid third-party API compatibility pattern
✅ Error transformation - User-facing error message at CLI boundary

### Files Reviewed
- `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py`: No violations

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-01 17:40:19 PT**: Error boundary pattern review - compliant. Exception handling at CLI level with proper context addition and chaining is allowed. String inspection for "404"/"Not Found" is acceptable for third-party API compatibility.

</details>", "body": "<!-- test-coverage-review -->
